title: Spark: A System for Scientifically Creative Idea Generation
text:
Abstract
Recently, large language models (LLMs) have shown promising abilities to generate novel research ideas in science, a direction which coincides with many foundational principles in computational creativity (CC). In light of these developments, we present an idea generation system named Spark that couples retrieval-augmented idea generation using LLMs with a reviewer model named Judge trained on 600K scientific reviews from OpenReview. Our work is both a system demonstration and intended to inspire other CC researchers to explore grounding the generation and evaluation of scientific ideas within foundational CC principles. To this end, we release the annotated dataset used to train Judge, inviting other researchers to explore the use of LLMs for idea generation and creative evaluations.

title: Spark: A System for Scientifically Creative Idea Generation

title: Transformational Creativity in Science: A Graphical Theory
text:
Abstract
Creative processes are typically divided into three types: combinatorial, exploratory, and transformational. Here, we provide a graphical theory of transformational scientific creativity, synthesizing Boden’s insight that transformational creativity arises from changes in the “enabling constraints” of a conceptual space and Kuhn’s structure of scientific revolutions as resulting from paradigm shifts. We prove that modifications made to axioms of our graphical model have the most transformative potential and then illustrate how several historical instances of transformational creativity can be captured by our framework.

title: Transformational Creativity in Science: A Graphical Theory
Samuel Schapiro
text:
Bio
  
  
    
     E-mail
  
  
    
     LinkedIn
  
  
      Google Scholar
  

  
  


Founding research scientist at a stealth AI company.



I am an interdisciplinary scholar studying the science of intelligence. I am currently interested in

Uncovering the fundamental, scientific principles of creativity governing minds and machines
Developing AI methods and systems that exhibit creative behavior

Previously, I was a researcher at the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing, Berkeley. I did my undergrad at the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign, where I studied theoretical computer science.




We are not students of some subject matter but students of problems. And problems may cut right across the border of any subject matter or discipline.
— Karl Popper, Conjectures and Refutations: The Growth of Scientific Knowledge (p. 141)

My research involves the study of problems that cut sharply across disciplinary boundaries. I believe insights from evolutionary computation and psychology, philosophy of science, and cognitive science are integral to developing creative and open-ended AI systems.


Goal: To uncover fundamental principles of creativity governing minds and machines. Achieved through a combination of conceptual and theoretical work [1], and empirical study of LLMs [2, 3].



S. Schapiro, J. Black, L. R. Varshney, Transformational Creativity in Science: A Graphical Theory, in the Proceedings of the 16th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 2025. [Best Short Paper Award]
S. Schapiro, A. Gladstone, J. Black, H. Ji. Do Semantic Distance Tests Actually Predict Creativity in LLMs?, Under Review at the 43rd International Conference on Machine Learning, 2026.
S. Schapiro, S. Shashidhar, A. Gladstone, J. Black, R. Moon, D. Hakkani-Tur, L. R. Varshney, Combinatorial Creativity: A New Frontier in Generalization Abilities, Under Review at 16th International Conference on Learning Representations, 2026.





Goal: To develop methods that make AI models more creative [4], and to apply these techniques to scientific discovery [5].



S. Schapiro, C. Francisco Park, F. Sosa, L. R. Varshney, CreativityNeuro: Steering Large Language Weights to Improve Divergent Thinking, Under Review at Third Conference on Language Modeling, 2026.
A. Sanyal, S. Schapiro, S. Shashidhar, R. Moon, L. R. Varshney, D. Hakkani-Tur, Spark: A System for Scientifically Creative Idea Generation, in the Proceedings of the 16th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 2025.







Reviewer at ICML 2026 Workshop on Creativity & Generative AI, ICML 2026, COLM 2026, NeurIPS 2026, NeurIPS 2024 Workshop on Creativity & Generative AI
